cases.name: markdown shape rewrites Content-Type and stamps x-markdown-tokens
json shape returns the v1 envelope with the documented fields
html accept passes the upstream body through unchanged

_notes: This example does not ship a docker-compose.yml, so
examples-smoke.sh skips it silently. The cases[] block documents
the assertions a hand-driven smoke run must pass: the markdown
and json shapes both rewrite Content-Type and stamp
x-markdown-tokens with a non-negative integer; the json shape's
body parses as JSON and carries schema_version="1" plus the
title / content_md / token_estimate / license / url / fetched_at
/ citation_required fields documented in README.md. The html
case asserts only the Content-Type because the runner does not
currently support headers_absent assertions; the README's curl
example shows that x-markdown-tokens is not stamped on the html
pass-through (no Markdown projection ran).
The body shape uses a single literal field (schema_version) so
the runner's deep_match exact-matches it without depending on
non-deterministic upstream values like fetched_at or token_estimate.
The full envelope contract is pinned in
e2e/tests/content_negotiation_e2e.rs (8 tests) and
e2e/tests/x_markdown_tokens_e2e.rs (5 tests), which exercise the
same negotiation pipeline against a static-action fixture so they
do not depend on a live upstream.
The upstream is test.sbproxy.dev, the workspace's hostname
placeholder; rewrite action.url in sb.yml to a real HTML page
before driving the cases end-to-end.
audit_check stays false until the OSS audit endpoint lands.
